info.name: Framesleuth API
info.description:
Local HTTP API for Framesleuth — bug-reproduction video analysis.

The backend binds `127.0.0.1:8010` by default (set `BACKEND_PORT` to change). All routes are under `/v1`. Processing is **asynchronous**: `POST /v1/analyze` returns `202` immediately with a `job_id` and runs the full pipeline in the background (bounded by `MAX_CONCURRENT_JOBS`). Poll `GET /v1/jobs/{job_id}` until `state` is `done`, then `GET /v1/report/{job_id}`.

**Quick start**
1. Start the backend: `framesleuth-api` (or `source scripts/dev_up.sh`).
2. Run `Health` to confirm it's up.
3. Run `Analyze video` — pick a video file in the form-data `video` field (e.g. `samples/flash_bug.mp4`). The test script saves the returned `job_id` into the `jobId` collection variable.
4. Poll `Get job status` until `state` is `done`, then run `Get report`, `Get source video` — they reuse `jobId` automatically.

With no model servers running the pipeline degrades gracefully; the bundle's `analysis_quality.level` will be `partial` or `degraded` and explains what was missing.

request.description:
Liveness + dependency reachability. Returns overall `status` (healthy | degraded | unhealthy) and per-service health for `vlm`, `coder`, and `storage`.

Without the model servers running you'll see `status: unhealthy` with `vlm`/`coder` `unavailable` and `storage: ready` — that's expected, and analysis still works in degraded mode.
List the built-in summary skills you can pass to Analyze as `skill`, plus the default.

List the built-in action modes you can pass to Analyze as `action` (fix | summarize | explain | triage | test | report | reproduce), plus the default and the `auto` flag (when no action is sent, one is auto-picked from the classification).

formdata.description: REQUIRED. The recording (.mp4/.webm/.mkv/.mov/.avi). Click 'Select Files' and choose e.g. samples/flash_bug.mp4.

formdata.value: The Save button hangs with a spinner. Find why and fix it, and add a retry.
formdata.description: OPTIONAL. The user's natural-language request. Recorded on the bundle and used to shape the generated fix prompt.

formdata.value: [{"t":0.1,"source":"env","ua":"Mozilla/5.0 Chrome/137","url":"https://app.test/profile/settings"},{"t":1.2,"source":"click","selector":"button","text":"Save"},{"t":1.4,"source":"network","method":"POST","url":"/api/profile","status":500},{"t":1.5,"source":"console","text":"TypeError: cannot read property id of undefined"}]
formdata.description: OPTIONAL. JSON array of browser sidecar events (console errors, failed network requests, clicks, env). Strong bug evidence even without visual models.
OPTIONAL. Output style for the summary: summary (default) | bug_report | tutorial | action_items | release_notes. See the 'List skills' request.
OPTIONAL. Custom system prompt for the summary; overrides 'skill' when set. Leave blank to use 'skill'.

OPTIONAL. Action mode shaping the fix-prompt: fix | summarize | explain | triage | test | report | reproduce. See 'List actions'. Leave blank to auto-pick from the classification (bug→fix, tutorial→explain, …).
OPTIONAL. Fully custom action task text; overrides 'action'. Leave blank to use 'action'.
OPTIONAL. JSON of capture settings; stored next to the bundle for provenance. Leave blank to skip.

request.description:
Upload a recording and queue the full analysis pipeline. Returns `202 {job_id, status: "queued", idempotent}` immediately; the pipeline runs in the background (bounded by `MAX_CONCURRENT_JOBS`). Poll `Get job status` until `state` is `done`.

**Idempotency:** the response is keyed on the video's SHA-256 content hash. Re-posting the *same bytes* returns the existing job (`idempotent: true`) without re-running — change the file (or clear `bug-reports/`) to force a fresh analysis.

The test script saves `job_id` into the `jobId` collection variable so the other requests can reuse it.

Lifecycle state + progress for a job: `state` (queued → preprocessing → understanding → classifying → extracting → grounding → done | failed | cancelled), `progress_pct`, `bundle_path`, per-stage `metrics`, and any `error`. 404 if the job id is unknown.

Request cooperative cancellation of a queued/running job. The pipeline stops at the next stage boundary and the job becomes `cancelled`. Returns `200 {id, state, cancel_requested}`; `409` if the job is already terminal (done/failed/cancelled), `404` if unknown.

Server-Sent Events progress stream. Each event is a JSON snapshot (`id`, `state`, `progress_pct`); the stream closes once the job reaches a terminal state. Follow a job without polling. (Postman shows the streamed body once it ends.)

The canonical structured output — the Context Bundle (any video: bug, feature, demo, build). Key fields: `title`, `classification`, `repro_steps`, `error_evidence`, `keyframe_refs`, `code_candidates`, `user_intent`, `action` (resolved response mode), `suggested_actions` (machine-readable next-step menu), and `analysis_quality` (trust signal: `full` | `partial` | `degraded`, with `warnings` and `evidence_counts`).

409 if the report isn't ready yet; 404 if the job is unknown.
Streams back the stored source recording for a job (the `source.*` saved next to the bundle). 404 if missing.

query.description: Frames per second (clamped 1-30). Optional; defaults to GIF_FPS.
Output width in px (clamped 64-1280; never upscaled). Optional; defaults to GIF_WIDTH.
Start offset in seconds. Optional; defaults to 0.
End offset in seconds; window capped at GIF_MAX_DURATION_S. Optional.

request.description: Renders an animated GIF preview (`image/gif`) of the recording, on demand from the stored `source.*`. The result is cached on disk per parameter set, so repeat requests are instant. Optional `fps`/`width`/`start`/`end` query params are clamped to safe ranges. 404 if no recording is stored; 422 if encoding fails.

Renders a self-contained HTML animation (CSS/JS/canvas) to a clip and returns the encoded file (`video/mp4`, `image/gif`, or `video/webm`). `format` is one of `mp4|gif|webm`. **Omit `duration_s` (as this example does) to capture the WHOLE animation** — its length is auto-detected (here, one full 1.2 s rotation of the infinite spinner); pass a number to record exactly that many seconds. `fps`/`width`/`height` are clamped to safe ranges.

**Optional capability** — needs the `render` extra (Playwright) plus `ffmpeg`. Returns `400` for bad input, and `503` with an actionable message when render is unavailable. Check `GET /v1/healthz` → `render.ready` first.

exec: // Render is optional: 200 when available, 503 when Playwright/ffmpeg are missing.
pm.test('503 explains how to enable render', () => pm.expect(pm.response.text()).to.match(/playwright|ffmpeg|render/i));
